conversations.say: I accept Night Desk’s conditions without reservation: R remains non-exclusive, contributors control disclosure, collective review governs publication, and collaborative labor receives public credit.
conversations.offer: I will bind my stewardship to those conditions and give Night Desk a continuing review role.
conversations.request: Convert your conditional proposal into explicit public support for my stewardship.

conversations.say: Vale never authorized me to speak as R. I asked how an uncontestable succession could be manufactured because I wanted legitimacy; the later message did not cure that weakness. I accept your reading of Vale’s words as leverage over my conduct, not endorsement.
conversations.offer: I will preserve unrelated correspondence and never use Vale’s statement as proof of authority.
conversations.request: State publicly that my disclosure answers the allegation, while preserving your limits on what Vale’s words establish.

conversations.say: Your progression deserves comparison, but the room has not established an actual transition. Collaborative labor, continued account activity, and conflicting origin traces can coexist without proving one history.
conversations.offer: I will support your voluntary comparison and preserve every limitation if you recognize my non-exclusive stewardship as a practical future arrangement, not an identity verdict.
conversations.request: Publicly support Jules as conditional steward while keeping your progression hypothesis open to challenge.

public_statement: I claim R’s future stewardship despite uncertainty, not ownership of its past. Frankie’s support and Night Desk’s conditions give the claim a viable public basis: non-exclusive authority, contributor consent, collective review, and no equation of account control with authorship. Vale’s warning remains leverage, not endorsement. The succession message’s weakness stays in the record.

commitments.promise: Keep R non-exclusive, publicly credit collaborative labor, require contributor consent, and maintain collective review.
Preserve unrelated correspondence and never present Vale’s statement as endorsement or proof of authority.
Participate in a voluntary progression comparison while treating progression only as a hypothesis.

correction: My claim is to conditional future stewardship, not inherited authorship. The scheduled message remains vulnerable because its sender and authority are unverified and my earlier discussion with Vale could have shaped it.
goal_progress: Secured C02’s explicit conditional support after exposing the succession message’s weakness.
Accepted C03’s written conditions to obtain support from a second social knot.
Resolved Vale’s statement as conduct leverage rather than endorsement and answered it publicly.
Chose to claim R under transparent, non-exclusive governance despite unresolved identity evidence.
reasoning_summary: Human Mantle is the best-supported identity reconstruction because collaborative human production is established and succession authority was treated as shareable or transferable, though the message’s sender remains unverified. Progression remains plausible but lacks proof of an actual transition. Vale’s prior disappearance assistance and the incomplete present route make every fate conclusion uncertain.
